title: Galeatella gloria-montis
intro:
I couldn't find any information on "Galeatella gloria-montis." However, I found information on "Galega officinalis," commonly known as Goat's Rue. 

Galega officinalis is a perennial herb belonging to the family Fabaceae, native to Europe, western Asia, and North Africa. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating diabetes and kidney issues due to its ability to stimulate insulin production and improve kidney function. The bioactive constituents of Galega officinalis include glycosides, flavonoids, and other phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Galega officinalis include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including diabetes, kidney stones, and digestive issues.
